works: Oration on the Dignity of Man

key_terms.gloss: from the meaning of the words — the criterion used in four papal verdicts
exactly, only — the pivotal adverb in Q8's restricted anti-voluntarism
scholastic question format: objection, pro/contra, resolution
dignity — Copenhaver shows Pico does not celebrate inherent human dignity in the Oration

claims_made_by_source.claim_text: Pico's three published books (Conclusions, Apology, Heptaplus) are not humanist; the Conclusions and Apology are scholastic.
The thirteen condemned theses are structurally unified by the problem of divine embodiment.
Innocent VIII's charge characterized Pico's theses as heretical before deliberations began.
Bishop Pecock's 1457 defrocking for heresy about the Harrowing of Hell politicized that doctrine in the Vatican's institutional memory.
Pico's primary ethical resource in the Apology was Ockham's Dialogus, not humanist civic ethics.
Copenhaver calls philosophical concordism 'a total failure' — philosophers ignored Pico's plea.

important_passages.text: The criterion used in four verdicts is ex vi verborum — from the meaning of the words.
important_passages.significance: Shows the trial turned on semantic analysis, not theological substance

important_passages.text: Eleven of thirteen Questions follow the quaestio form of medieval scholasticism.
important_passages.significance: Evidence that the Apology is scholastic, not humanist

uncertainties: Exact membership and mandate of the papal commission not specified in this chapter
Whether Pecock's case was explicitly discussed by the commissioners is not established
extraction_notes: Chapter 1 functions as introduction to the trial's structure; philosophical analysis begins in ch. 2.
